threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think 'the Tube' is the only way to get around. Meanwhile, locals are all about the buses, especially the 38.
Caught the 103 late last night, and the driver serenaded us with old soul classics. Never knew a bus could feel like home.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a busker playing accordion to a crowd of joggers at Tempelhofer Feld. That blend of chaos and calm felt like home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg do you find spontaneous dance parties on the U-Bahn platforms, where commuters transform into dancers at a moment's notice. Pure Berlin.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local by their old-school preference for the 14 over the new train lines. We know the 14 has soul, even when it’s late.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 47 this morning, driver offered a doughnut to a regular who forgot his fare. Just another day in the city, right?

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is just about Gaudí, but the real charm lies in the quiet plazas where locals sip cafès and gossip. They miss the heart.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell by the way they ask for directions to Parc Güell—like it’s the only place they’ve heard of.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it the 'tube'. Locals just hop on the 'overground' like it’s the only option, eyes rolling at your touristy lingo.
Caught a night bus from Peckham and overheard a bloke raving about how he saw a pigeon steal fries from a toddler. Classic London!

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The day I heard a neighbor playing accordion while waiting for the bus, and another joined in with a song. That’s Neukölln magic.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain can you get a kebab at 3 AM after a night out, and the vendor still remembers your order from last week.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s from here when they know the best taco truck isn’t La Taqueria, but the one tucked behind the park at 24th.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy in full tuxedo, tails and all, ride the 47 home after a wedding. That’s classic SF—where glamour meets grit on the same bus.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists never notice the small streets in Gràcia are alive at night, but only when the locals gather for their secret barbecues on rooftops.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a newcomer by the way they still stare in awe at the Casa Batlló while everyone else just rushes by.
